project_summary: pact is a graph-first structural verifier that finds violations at component interfaces by treating code as G=(V,E) where violations are paths violating constraint predicates C(p), not AST pattern matches. It orchestrates Z3, Hypothesis, TLA+, and CrossHair to formally verify load-bearing functions, then uses self-improving LLM prompts to synthesize minimal patches that restore contracts.

understanding.purpose: Unified LLM client factory providing consistent Anthropic SDK instantiation across pact's 9 LLM-calling modules (intent.py, spec_learner.py, etc.). Module abstracts multi-provider auth (Anthropic direct with x-api-key header, OpenRouter proxy via PACT_LLM_BASE_URL, Bonsai bearer-token proxy via ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN) and model selection (resolve_model with PACT_LLM_MODEL override). Git history shows module evolved from single-provider (Anthropic-only) to multi-provider over 2 days (2026-05-25 to 2026-05-26) through 3 fix cycles addressing: dotenv import-time side effects, OpenRouter URL path construction, and key priority ordering. Current stable state (all 611 tests pass per git message) provides make_client() factory returning anthropic.Anthropic client, resolve_key() extracting key string for external use, resolve_model() for model name resolution. Module has no external data dependencies (no file I/O, no network calls) — purely environment-variable configuration facade.
understanding.design_intent: Design prioritizes compatibility with three LLM provider workflows: (1) Anthropic direct API (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var, x-api-key header), (2) OpenRouter multi-model proxy (PACT_LLM_BASE_URL + PACT_LLM_API_KEY, custom base URL with x-api-key header), (3) Bonsai/OAuth bearer-token proxies (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL + ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN, SDK reads bearer token from env directly). Key design choice: PACT_LLM_* variables take priority over ANTHROPIC_* to prevent shell-environment Anthropic keys from shadowing proxy keys (git history: 'so proxy keys aren't shadowed by a rate-limited Anthropic key'). This solves problem where developers with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in .bashrc hit rate limits but want to route through unlimited proxy. Second design choice: defer 'import anthropic' to make_client call-time (line 27) not module-level, allowing llm.py import when anthropic package not installed (test environments using mocks). Third: resolve_key returns empty string when ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN present but no x-api-key available (lines 62-66) — signals SDK to use bearer auth, but creates implicit coupling between resolve_key return value and SDK environment-reading behavior.
understanding.key_abstractions: visible_definitions: [DEFAULT_MODEL, _KEY_HELP, make_client, resolve_key, resolve_model]. DEFAULT_MODEL (line 8): string constant 'claude-sonnet-4-6', default model when PACT_LLM_MODEL unset and model parameter None. _KEY_HELP (lines 10-17): multi-line help string documenting three provider setups (Anthropic direct, OpenRouter, Bonsai proxy), raised in RuntimeError when no auth configured; explicitly specifies OpenRouter base URL as https://openrouter.ai/api (not /api/v1) because Anthropic SDK appends /v1/messages to base_url. make_client (lines 20-47): factory function taking optional api_key parameter, resolves key via 5-tier fallback (param → PACT_LLM_API_KEY → PACT_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY → ANTHROPIC_API_KEY → ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN implicit), resolves base_url via 2-tier (PACT_LLM_BASE_URL → ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL), builds kwargs dict conditionally (only include api_key if non-empty, only include base_url if set), returns anthropic.Anthropic(**kw) instance; raises RuntimeError if no auth source available. resolve_key (lines 50-66): standalone key resolver, same 5-tier fallback as make_client but returns string '' when ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN sole auth (enables SDK bearer-auth fallback); used by callers needing key string for non-SDK purposes. resolve_model (lines 69-71): model name resolver, 3-tier fallback (param → PACT_LLM_MODEL env → DEFAULT_MODEL), always returns non-None string.
understanding.behavioral_contract: make_client contract: (1) precondition: at least one of {api_key param, PACT_LLM_API_KEY, PACT_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN} must be set, else raises RuntimeError(_KEY_HELP) at line 39-40; (2) postcondition: returns anthropic.Anthropic instance configured with resolved api_key (if key non-empty) and base_url (if either PACT_LLM_BASE_URL or ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL set); (3) side effect: imports anthropic module on first call (line 27 deferred import). resolve_key contract: (1) precondition: same as make_client (at least one auth source); (2) postcondition: returns str, either non-empty API key string or '' (when ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN sole source); (3) no side effects, pure environment read. resolve_model contract: (1) precondition: none (always safe to call); (2) postcondition: returns str, never None (DEFAULT_MODEL ensures non-None); (3) no side effects. All three functions read os.environ but do not modify it. No file I/O, no network calls, no async operations. Auth priority documented in make_client docstring but discrepancy: docstring lists ANTHROPIC_API_KEY second, code checks it fourth (after PACT_LLM_API_KEY and PACT_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY).
understanding.failure_modes: No try/except blocks visible — all exceptions propagate to caller. Failure mode 1: ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN present but invalid/expired — resolve_key returns '' successfully but SDK raises authentication error when client used, far from llm.py call site (delayed failure, hard to diagnose). Failure mode 2: anthropic package not installed — make_client line 27 raises ImportError, but only on first client instantiation not module import (deferred import design hides missing dependency until runtime). Failure mode 3: PACT_LLM_BASE_URL set to invalid URL (e.g., https://openrouter.ai/api/v1 with double /v1/ per git history fix) — SDK silently constructs malformed request URL, 404 at network layer. Failure mode 4: multiple auth sources set with conflicting validity (e.g., PACT_LLM_API_KEY valid but ANTHROPIC_API_KEY rate-limited) — priority order prevents rate-limited key from shadowing valid key (design intent) but developer may not realize which key is used. Failure mode 5: resolve_key called in environment with no auth, catches same RuntimeError as make_client but caller expecting string return may not anticipate exception.
understanding.assumptions: Assumes anthropic SDK reads ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN directly from os.environ for bearer auth (not passed via kwargs) — resolve_key relies on this SDK behavior when returning '' (lines 62-66). Assumes PACT_LLM_BASE_URL and ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL are valid HTTP(S) URLs when set — no validation before passing to SDK. Assumes Anthropic SDK appends /v1/messages to base_url — _KEY_HELP line 13 documents OpenRouter base as https://openrouter.ai/api not .../api/v1 because SDK path-building adds /v1/ (git history: 'SDK appends /v1/messages to base_url'). Assumes all env var values are strings — no type checking on os.environ.get() results. Assumes DEFAULT_MODEL 'claude-sonnet-4-6' is valid Anthropic model identifier accepted by SDK. Assumes callers of resolve_key understand '' return value means bearer-auth mode (docstring mentions ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN but doesn't explain SDK coupling). Assumes anthropic package installed at make_client call time — deferred import (line 27) moves ImportError from module load to function call.
understanding.resource_obligations: No file handles, network sockets, subprocesses, or async tasks created. No transactions or locks. Obligation 1: defer anthropic import until make_client called (line 27) — satisfied, import inside function not module-level. Obligation 2: read os.environ without modifying — satisfied, all os.environ.get() calls are reads. Obligation 3: return anthropic.Anthropic instance that SDK manages — caller responsible for SDK client lifecycle (no cleanup in this module). Obligation 4: document multi-provider setup in _KEY_HELP — satisfied (lines 10-17 cover three providers). Git history shows dotenv obligation removed: 'moved load_dotenv call into cli.py::main() so it only fires on CLI entry' — llm.py no longer loads .env file, delegates to CLI layer. No resource cleanup required (no __enter__/__exit__, no close() methods).

invariants.statement: make_client and resolve_key enforce identical 5-tier auth priority: explicit api_key parameter, then PACT_LLM_API_KEY, then PACT_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, then ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, then ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN fallback. PACT_LLM_API_KEY takes priority over ANTHROPIC_API_KEY so proxy keys aren't shadowed by shell environment's Anthropic key.
invariants.formal: ∀ call ∈ {make_client, resolve_key}: key_resolution_order = [param, PACT_LLM_API_KEY, PACT_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN] ∧ pact_keys_precede(anthropic_keys)
invariants.derived_from: make_client lines 29-34, resolve_key lines 57-63; git history: 'PACT_LLM_API_KEY now takes priority over ANTHROPIC_API_KEY so proxy keys aren't shadowed by a rate-limited Anthropic key'

invariants.statement: make_client base_url resolution prioritizes PACT_LLM_BASE_URL over ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL (line 35-37). PACT_LLM_BASE_URL enables OpenRouter/proxy routing while ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL is SDK-native override.
invariants.derived_from: Line 35-37 base_url resolution; git history: 'PACT_LLM_BASE_URL likewise takes priority over ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL'

invariants.statement: resolve_key returns empty string '' when no API key in environment but ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN present (lines 62-66). This signals Anthropic SDK to use bearer auth via ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN header it reads directly, supporting Bonsai proxy and OAuth flows.
invariants.derived_from: resolve_key lines 62-66: fallback to '' when no key found, only raise if ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN also absent; _KEY_HELP line 15-16 documents bearer token usage

invariants.statement: make_client and resolve_key raise RuntimeError with _KEY_HELP message when no auth configured: no api_key parameter, no PACT_LLM_API_KEY, no PACT_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, and no ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN in environment (make_client line 39-40, resolve_key line 64-65).
invariants.derived_from: make_client line 39-40, resolve_key line 64-65; _KEY_HELP lines 10-17 provides setup instructions for three provider types

invariants.statement: resolve_model returns model parameter if non-None/non-empty, else PACT_LLM_MODEL env var, else DEFAULT_MODEL constant 'claude-sonnet-4-6' (line 71). Three-tier fallback ensures callers always get valid model name.
invariants.formal: ∀ resolve_model_call: return(model ∨ PACT_LLM_MODEL ∨ DEFAULT_MODEL) ∧ return_value ≠ None
invariants.derived_from: resolve_model line 71 uses or-chaining; DEFAULT_MODEL line 8 = 'claude-sonnet-4-6'

invariants.statement: make_client imports anthropic at function call time (line 27) rather than module level. This defers anthropic SDK dependency until first LLM client instantiation, allowing module import when anthropic package not installed (e.g., test environments using mocks).
invariants.derived_from: Line 27 'import anthropic' inside make_client body; no anthropic import in module-level imports (lines 3-6)

invariants.statement: _KEY_HELP constant (lines 10-17) documents setup for three provider types: Anthropic direct (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY with x-api-key header), OpenRouter (PACT_LLM_BASE_URL=https://openrouter.ai/api + PACT_LLM_API_KEY), Bonsai bearer proxy (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=https://go.trybons.ai + ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN). OpenRouter URL explicitly /api not /api/v1 because SDK appends /v1/messages.
invariants.derived_from: _KEY_HELP lines 12-16; git history: 'correct OpenRouter base URL in help text (/api not /api/v1) — SDK appends /v1/messages, so /api/v1 produces double /v1/'

invariants.statement: make_client builds anthropic.Anthropic kwargs dict conditionally: api_key included only if key resolved non-empty (lines 43-44), base_url included only if either PACT_LLM_BASE_URL or ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL set (lines 45-46). When ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN used (key=''), api_key omitted from kwargs so SDK reads bearer token from environment directly.
invariants.formal: ∀ make_client_call: kw = {} ∧ (key → kw['api_key']=key) ∧ (base_url → kw['base_url']=base_url) ∧ (¬key ∧ ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN → 'api_key' ∉ kw)
invariants.derived_from: Lines 42-47 conditional dict building; line 29-34 key resolution; line 39-40 permits empty key when ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN present

understanding.purpose: Module constructs directed call graph from extracted function manifests and call sites (line 4-6: 'nodes are qualified function names and edges are call relationships') to enable dataflow queries across module boundaries. Primary use case (line 6): trace whether None values can propagate to NOT NULL database fields via reachability analysis. Provides three query operations: reachable_from (transitive callees via nx.descendants line 51), callers_of (immediate predecessors line 58), call_sites_to (call sites with line numbers/context via edge metadata lines 66-69). Degrades gracefully when networkx unavailable (try/except ImportError lines 14-19, _HAS_NX flag gates all graph operations) returning empty results with RuntimeWarning instead of raising ImportError. Git history 2026-05-25 added _require_graph warnings to surface infrastructure failures ('networkx not installed' vs 'graph never built') that previously returned silent empty results.
understanding.design_intent: Graph construction (build_call_graph lines 73-98) separates internal functions (from functions list, stored as nodes with manifest attribute line 86) from external calls (implicitly created nodes without manifest). Resolves unqualified call names (lines 84-88 short_to_qual dictionary maps 'foo' → 'module.foo') to handle imports like 'from module import foo' where call site uses short name. Sentinel '__root__' node (line 91) tracks module-level calls enabling entry-point analysis. Edge metadata stores CallSite objects (line 96) preserving source location for 'show me where function X is called' queries. _require_graph guard (lines 26-44) centralizes two failure modes: (1) networkx not installed (dependency optional for lightweight deployments), (2) CallGraph constructed but build_call_graph never called (API misuse). stacklevel=3 in warnings (lines 33,41) attempts to attribute warnings to user code not library internals assuming 3-frame call depth. Design trades off ambiguous name resolution (first match wins via setdefault line 88, no collision warning) for simplicity.
understanding.key_abstractions: CallGraph dataclass (lines 22-70): wrapper around networkx.DiGraph with _g: object field (line 24, type hint accommodates None when networkx missing). _HAS_NX module constant (lines 14-19): bool flag set via try/except ImportError, gates all networkx API calls. _require_graph method (lines 26-44): precondition checker returning bool, emits RuntimeWarning for missing dependency or uninitialized graph, called by all three query methods (lines 47,54,61). reachable_from (lines 46-51): wraps nx.descendants for transitive closure of callees. callers_of (lines 53-58): wraps DiGraph.predecessors for immediate callers. call_sites_to (lines 60-70): iterates in_edges with data=True (line 66), extracts 'call_site' attribute (line 67), filters None (line 68), returns list[CallSite] with source locations. build_call_graph function (lines 73-98): factory taking functions list and call_sites list, constructs DiGraph with nodes per function (line 86 stores manifest), edges per call (line 96 stores call_site), returns CallGraph wrapper. short_to_qual dict (lines 84-88): maps unqualified names to first matching qualified name via setdefault, enables resolution of 'from X import Y; Y()' call patterns.
understanding.behavioral_contract: build_call_graph contract: given functions list and call_sites list, returns CallGraph where (1) nodes = {f.name for f in functions} ∪ {external calls}, (2) edges = {(caller, callee, call_site) for call in call_sites}, (3) unqualified callee names resolved via short_to_qual.get (lines 93-95), (4) calls without caller_name attributed to '__root__' (line 91). Returns CallGraph(_g=None) if networkx unavailable (line 78) with no error raised. Query methods contract: all return list (never None, never raise when node missing), empty list when _require_graph fails (lines 48,55,62) or node not in graph (lines 49,56,63). _require_graph contract: returns True if _HAS_NX and self._g is not None, else warns and returns False (lines 28-43). reachable_from contract: returns transitive callees via nx.descendants (line 51), order unspecified. callers_of contract: returns immediate predecessors (line 58), order unspecified. call_sites_to contract: returns CallSite objects for in-edges (lines 66-69), filters out edges missing call_site attribute (line 68).
understanding.failure_modes: (1) Ambiguous name resolution (lines 84-88): if functions=['a.foo', 'b.foo'], short_to_qual maps 'foo'→'a.foo' (first wins via setdefault line 88), unqualified call 'foo()' always resolves to 'a.foo' even if caller intended 'b.foo'. No collision warning. Order-dependent: changing functions list order changes resolution. (2) External node manifest access (line 96 implicit node creation): G.add_edge for call to external function (e.g., 'json.loads') creates node without manifest attribute (only functions loop line 86 sets manifest). Accessing external_node['manifest'] raises KeyError. No documented way to distinguish internal vs external nodes. (3) stacklevel=3 brittleness (lines 33,41): warnings.warn assumes user_code→query_method→_require_graph call depth. Refactoring query methods to add helper breaks assumption, warning points to internal code. (4) Silent fallback (line 78): if _HAS_NX False, build_call_graph returns CallGraph(_g=None), all queries return [] with warning on first access. Caller cannot distinguish 'graph is empty' from 'networkx unavailable' without checking warnings.
understanding.assumptions: (1) networkx optional dependency: module must function (degraded mode returning empty results) when networkx not installed (lines 14-19,28-34). (2) Qualified function names unique: functions list has no duplicate f.name values (no deduplication in lines 85-88, duplicate names overwrite node manifest). (3) FunctionManifest has .name attribute: line 86 accesses f.name without validation. (4) CallSite has .caller_name and .callee_name attributes: lines 91-92 access without validation. (5) caller_name may be None: line 91 'call.caller_name or "__root__"' handles None. (6) networkx.DiGraph API stable: lines 51,58,66 call nx.descendants, .predecessors, .in_edges without version check. (7) Short name extraction via split('.')[-1] (line 87) correct: assumes qualified names are dot-separated (breaks for 'functools::lru_cache' or other separators). (8) call_site attribute name collision: assumes 'call_site' not used by other graph algorithms (line 96 edge attribute, line 67 retrieval).
understanding.resource_obligations: No file handles, database connections, or async resources. networkx.DiGraph (line 80) held in memory, size O(nodes + edges) where nodes=len(functions)+external_calls, edges=len(call_sites). No explicit cleanup: CallGraph._g persists until garbage collected. nx.descendants (line 51) performs graph traversal O(nodes+edges) worst case (full reachability). in_edges iteration (line 66) O(in_degree(func_name)). No timeouts, no resource limits. Memory obligation: caller responsible for not building graph for arbitrarily large codebases (e.g., 100K functions, 1M call sites would consume GBs). No streaming or pagination: build_call_graph materializes full graph in memory (line 80 G = nx.DiGraph(), lines 85-96 populate all nodes/edges before returning).

invariants.statement: Module degrades gracefully when networkx unavailable: _HAS_NX flag (lines 14-19) set False on ImportError, build_call_graph returns CallGraph(_g=None) (line 78), all query methods (_require_graph lines 26-44) check _HAS_NX and warn 'networkx is not installed — graph queries return empty results' (lines 30-31) before returning []. Contract: module never raises ImportError to callers, all methods return empty results ([], never None) when dependency missing.
invariants.derived_from: Lines 14-19 try/except ImportError; line 28 check not _HAS_NX; lines 47,54,61 early return [] when _require_graph() False; line 78 return CallGraph(_g=None)

invariants.statement: CallGraph methods require _g is not None: _require_graph (lines 26-44) returns False if self._g is None (line 36) warning 'graph was never built — call build_call_graph() first' (lines 38-39). All query methods (reachable_from, callers_of, call_sites_to) guard with 'if not self._require_graph(): return []' (lines 47,54,61). Contract: constructing CallGraph with _g=None is valid but all queries return [] until build_call_graph() called.
invariants.formal: ∀ cg: CallGraph: cg._g = None → ∀ query_method: query_method(cg, _) = []
invariants.derived_from: Lines 36-43 check self._g is None; lines 47-48, 54-55, 61-62 guard pattern; line 78 valid to return CallGraph(_g=None)

invariants.statement: build_call_graph (lines 73-98) resolves unqualified callee names to qualified names via short_to_qual dictionary (lines 84-88): for each function, extracts short name via split('.')[-1] (line 87), maps short→qualified using setdefault (line 88). When processing call_sites (lines 90-96), if target not in func_names (line 93), falls back to short_to_qual.get(short, target) (line 95). Invariant: unqualified call 'foo()' from module.bar matches first function with short name 'foo' inserted into short_to_qual; if multiple 'foo' definitions exist, first one wins (setdefault semantics). Risk: ambiguous resolution if 'pkg1.foo' and 'pkg2.foo' both present.
invariants.derived_from: Lines 84-88 short_to_qual construction with setdefault; lines 93-95 fallback resolution logic

invariants.statement: build_call_graph treats calls without caller_name as originating from '__root__' sentinel (line 91): 'source = call.caller_name or "__root__"'. Allows top-level module code calls to be tracked in graph. Graph structure: __root__ node has out-edges to module-level function calls, enabling reachability queries from module entry point.
invariants.formal: ∀ call ∈ call_sites: call.caller_name = None → graph.add_edge('__root__', call.callee_name, call_site=call)
invariants.derived_from: Line 91 source = call.caller_name or '__root__'

invariants.statement: Graph edges store CallSite objects as metadata: G.add_edge(source, target, call_site=call) (line 96). call_sites_to method (lines 60-70) retrieves via in_edges(func_name, data=True) (line 66) then data.get('call_site') (line 67). Invariant: each edge carries original CallSite enabling line number/context recovery for 'who calls this function' queries. Missing call_site data filtered out (lines 67-68 'if site').
invariants.formal: ∀ edge(s,t): ∃ call ∈ call_sites: edge.data['call_site'] = call ∧ call.caller_name = s ∧ call.callee_name = t
invariants.derived_from: Line 96 call_site=call edge attribute; lines 66-69 retrieval and filtering logic

invariants.statement: Query methods (reachable_from, callers_of, call_sites_to) check 'if func_name not in self._g: return []' (lines 49,56,63) after _require_graph guard. Contract: querying nonexistent node returns [] (empty result) not KeyError. Enables safe querying of external functions (e.g., stdlib calls) that won't be in func_names set.
invariants.derived_from: Lines 49-50, 56-57, 63-64 membership check pattern

invariants.statement: build_call_graph stores FunctionManifest objects as node attributes: G.add_node(f.name, manifest=f) (line 86). Enables downstream consumers to retrieve function metadata (parameters, return type, docstring, etc.) from graph nodes without separate lookup. Access pattern not visible in this module but implied by attribute storage.
invariants.derived_from: Line 86 manifest=f node attribute

invariants.statement: _require_graph emits RuntimeWarning (not UserWarning or DeprecationWarning) for missing networkx (line 32) and uninitialized graph (line 40) with stacklevel=3 (lines 33,41). stacklevel=3 makes warning appear to originate from caller's caller (e.g., if user code calls cg.reachable_from which calls _require_graph, warning points to user code line not internal method). Design choice: use RuntimeWarning category (indicates runtime environment issue not code issue) for infrastructure failures.
invariants.derived_from: Lines 29-34 networkx warning, lines 37-42 unbuilt graph warning, both RuntimeWarning with stacklevel=3

understanding.purpose: context.py extracts violation signals from git history, changelog, and inline code comments to feed pact find as a prior, replacing uniform sampling with real-world failure signals. Module docstring (lines 1-8) describes three data sources: git history for commits mentioning fixes/edge cases, changelog for documented failures, inline TODO/FIXME/HACK notes. Extract_context (lines 209-281) orchestrates collection via _git_log/_changelog/_inline_notes, sends aggregated text to LLM via _call, parses JSON response, optionally writes to file. Self-improvement: _improve_context_prompt (lines 140-206) rewrites prompt template if LLM output empty despite rich git history.
understanding.design_intent: Design prioritizes signal-to-noise ratio over completeness. _git_log (lines 50-76) filters 200 commits to only those matching _FIX_PATTERN (line 27 regex: 'fix|bug|crash|edge.?case|off.?by.?one|overflow|...'), retrieves diffs for top 15, truncates each to 1500 chars then total to 8000 — design assumes fixes cluster in recent commits and diff headers contain enough context. _changelog (lines 79-96) matches file stem OR _FIX_PATTERN in any line, returns first 80 — design assumes stem appears in relevant entries, but no disambiguation (e.g. 'utils.py' matches any 'utils' mention). _run (lines 35-47) suppresses all subprocess exceptions, returns empty string — design prioritizes availability over diagnostics (caller treats empty as 'no data' not 'command failed'). _improve_context_prompt (lines 140-206) triggers only if parse_error OR (git_log > 500 chars AND 0 violations) — design assumes rich history with zero output signals prompt deficiency, but no scoring of non-empty low-quality output.
understanding.key_abstractions: visible_definitions: [_PROMPT_DIR, _DEFAULT_MODEL, _SYSTEM, _FIX_PATTERN, _run, _git_log, _changelog, _inline_notes, _parse, _call, _improve_context_prompt, extract_context, main]. _PROMPT_DIR (line 20): Path to prompts/ directory, resolved relative to __file__. _DEFAULT_MODEL (line 21): string 'claude-sonnet-4-6', fallback when PACT_LLM_MODEL unset. _SYSTEM (lines 22-25): LLM system prompt enforcing JSON-only output. _FIX_PATTERN (lines 27-32): compiled regex matching 17 bug-related keywords with word boundaries, case-insensitive. _run (lines 35-47): subprocess wrapper accepting cmd list, cwd, timeout (default 30), returns stdout string or '' on any exception. _git_log (lines 50-76): extracts git log for file, filters to commits matching _FIX_PATTERN, retrieves diffs for top 15, truncates. _changelog (lines 79-96): searches 5 changelog filenames in repo root, returns lines matching file stem or _FIX_PATTERN. _inline_notes (lines 99-110): extracts lines matching TODO|FIXME|HACK|XXX|BUG|NOTE. _parse (lines 113-123): strips markdown fences, attempts JSON parse on text or text after first '{', raises RuntimeError with text[:300] if both fail. _call (lines 126-137): imports llm.make_client, creates Anthropic client, sends prompt, parses response. _improve_context_prompt (lines 140-206): evaluates result quality, if poor despite rich history, calls LLM with context_improve.md template, writes improved prompt to context.md. extract_context (lines 209-281): orchestrates collection, LLM call, optional file write, optional prompt improvement. main (lines 284-318): argparse CLI, calls extract_context.
understanding.behavioral_contract:
extract_context contract (lines 209-281): accepts file_path (required), repo_root (optional, auto-detected via .git search), model (default _DEFAULT_MODEL), api_key (optional, resolved via llm.resolve_key), output (optional Path), verbose (bool), improve (bool). Returns dict with 'confirmed_violations' and 'fragile_areas' keys (or stub with 'file' key if _call fails). Writes to output path if provided (line 275-276). Calls _improve_context_prompt if improve=True (line 278-279). _git_log contract (lines 50-76): returns string of git diffs for commits matching _FIX_PATTERN, max 8000 chars, or '(no git history)' if no commits or empty log. _changelog contract (lines 79-96): returns '
'-joined lines matching stem or _FIX_PATTERN, max 80 lines, or '(no changelog found)' if no changelog file exists. _inline_notes contract (lines 99-110): returns formatted 'line NNNN: text' strings for lines matching TODO|FIXME|HACK|XXX|BUG|NOTE, or '(none)' if no matches, or '(unreadable)' if file read fails. _run contract (lines 35-47): returns stdout string on success, empty string '' on ANY exception (timeout, CalledProcessError, OSError) — no distinction between 'command succeeded with empty output' and 'command failed'. _parse contract (lines 113-123): returns dict parsed from JSON text, raises RuntimeError with text[:300] if parsing fails after stripping markdown and trying text or text-after-first-brace.
understanding.failure_modes: _run (lines 35-47) silent failure mode: line 46 'except Exception: return ""' suppresses timeout (line 42), CalledProcessError from check=True (line 43), and OSError (cwd not found, git not installed). Caller receives '' for both 'git log empty' and 'git command failed', cannot distinguish. _parse (lines 113-123) truncated diagnostic: line 123 RuntimeError includes text[:300], if LLM preamble > 300 chars before JSON, error message omits actual JSON body. _improve_context_prompt (lines 176-206) non-atomic write: line 191 write_text with no fsync or temp-file-rename pattern, concurrent writes or crashes corrupt context.md. _improve_context_prompt (lines 196-206) swallowed exception with warning only: warnings.warn (line 199) logs failure but does not prevent caller from proceeding with stale prompt. extract_context (lines 250-260) malformed stub result: on _call exception, constructs dict with 'file' key (line 257) instead of expected 'confirmed_violations'/'fragile_areas' schema, downstream _improve_context_prompt (line 279) receives malformed input. Git commit [2026-05-21] states 'add check=True so failed git/other commands raise on non-zero exit' but _run still suppresses via except Exception.
understanding.assumptions: _git_log assumes _FIX_PATTERN regex (line 27) captures bug-related commits, but pattern may miss domain-specific terms (e.g. 'regression', 'flaky', 'race') or match false positives (e.g. 'workaround' in feature addition). _git_log assumes 1500-char truncation (line 74) preserves violation context, breaks if fix is in truncated diff region. _changelog assumes file stem appears in relevant changelog entries (line 94 'stem in ln'), breaks if changelog uses different naming (e.g. 'context module' vs 'context.py'). _inline_notes assumes tags are complete words (line 101 \b boundary), misses 'TODO:' without space. _parse assumes markdown fences are ``` only (line 115), misses ~~~. _call assumes llm.make_client returns anthropic.Anthropic with .messages.create method (lines 129-135), breaks if llm module changes interface. _improve_context_prompt assumes git_log_len > 500 signals 'rich history' (line 153), arbitrary threshold. extract_context assumes repo root has .git directory (line 225), breaks for git worktrees or bare repos.
understanding.resource_obligations: extract_context (lines 209-281) reads multiple files: (_PROMPT_DIR / 'context.md') at line 241, output path at lines 275-276 if provided, file_path for _inline_notes at line 234. No file handles held open — all use Path.read_text/write_text which open/close automatically. _run (lines 35-47) spawns subprocess at line 37, subprocess.run blocks until completion or timeout (default 30s), no explicit cleanup needed (subprocess module handles). _call (lines 126-137) imports llm module at line 127, creates anthropic.Anthropic client at line 129, sends API request at line 130. Anthropic SDK uses httpx connection pooling, client reused across calls if same key, no explicit close method. Obligation: caller should reuse client for multiple extract_context calls to same API endpoint, but module creates new client per _call invocation (line 129 make_client(key)) — inefficient for batch processing. _improve_context_prompt (lines 140-206) writes context.md at line 191, no file handle held — write_text opens/writes/closes atomically at Python level but not OS level (no fsync). No temp file used, no rollback on partial write.

invariants.statement: _run (lines 35-47) catches ALL exceptions (line 46: except Exception) and returns empty string, suppressing subprocess failures including timeout, CalledProcessError, and OSError. Caller cannot distinguish 'git command succeeded with empty output' from 'git not installed' or 'timeout expired'.
invariants.derived_from: Line 46 bare except Exception with unconditional return ''

invariants.statement: _git_log (lines 50-76) truncates git show output to 1500 chars per commit (line 74), then joins and truncates to 8000 total (line 76). If fix_shas contains 15 commits at max 1500 each (22500 chars), result is truncated to 8000. Git diff context is arbitrarily lost — cannot guarantee violation signal preserved if fix is in truncated region.
invariants.formal: ∀ commits, max_len: len(commits) * 1500 > 8000 → ∃ violation_signal ∈ commits: truncated(violation_signal)
invariants.derived_from: Lines 74 diff[:1500], 76 join(out)[:8000] — arbitrary truncation boundaries

invariants.statement: _changelog (lines 79-96) searches for file stem OR _FIX_PATTERN anywhere in line (line 94), returns first 80 matching lines. If stem is common word ('utils', 'core') or _FIX_PATTERN is broad (matches 'prevent', 'handle'), false positives dominate. No temporal ordering or relevance scoring.
invariants.formal: ∀ file_stem, changelog_text: common_word(file_stem) → ∃ false_positive ∈ matches: ¬relevant(false_positive, file_stem)
invariants.derived_from: Line 94 'stem in ln or _FIX_PATTERN.search(ln)' with no context filtering

invariants.statement: _parse (lines 113-123) attempts JSON parsing twice (line 118-122 loop over two candidates), raises RuntimeError with truncated text (text[:300]) if both fail. If LLM output is valid JSON but > 300 chars before first '{', error message contains no JSON and obscures actual failure mode.
invariants.formal: ∀ text: '{' ∉ text[:300] ∧ valid_json(text[300:]) → RuntimeError.message contains no diagnostic
invariants.derived_from: Line 123 RuntimeError includes text[:300] only, may miss JSON body

invariants.statement: _call (line 126-137) imports llm.make_client at runtime (line 127 'from .llm import make_client'), defers ImportError to call-time. If llm module or anthropic SDK unavailable, error surfaces during extract_context execution not at module load, obscuring configuration issues until first LLM call.
invariants.derived_from: Line 127 local import, not module-level

invariants.statement: _improve_context_prompt (lines 140-206) writes to filesystem (line 191 _PROMPT_DIR / 'context.md') without backup, file locking, or atomic write. If process crashes between write and flush, context.md corrupted. If concurrent pact processes run, last write wins with no merge conflict detection.
invariants.formal: ∀ concurrent_writes: write(context.md, p1) ∧ write(context.md, p2) → lost_update(p1) ∨ lost_update(p2)
invariants.derived_from: Line 191 direct write_text with no locking or atomicity

invariants.statement: _improve_context_prompt (lines 140-206) emits RuntimeWarning (lines 199-204) when prompt improvement fails but continues execution. Git history commit [2026-05-25] states 'Now emits RuntimeWarning regardless of verbosity' as fix for silent exception swallowing, but warnings.warn does not prevent caller from proceeding with stale context.md.
invariants.derived_from: Lines 196-206 except Exception as exc: warnings.warn, no re-raise

invariants.statement: extract_context (lines 209-281) imports llm.resolve_key at runtime (line 218 'from .llm import resolve_key'), defers ImportError until function invocation. If llm module unavailable, error occurs after argparse validation, wasting user time on CLI argument processing before inevitable failure.
invariants.derived_from: Line 218 local import after function entry

invariants.statement: extract_context (lines 209-281) catches exception during _call (lines 250-260), sets parse_error and continues with stub result containing 'file' key (line 257). Downstream code in _improve_context_prompt (line 279) receives malformed result with 'file' instead of expected 'confirmed_violations' structure, but no schema validation enforced.
invariants.derived_from: Lines 256-260 stub result with 'file' key, no validation before line 279 call

invariants.statement: extract_context (lines 209-281) writes output to file (lines 274-276) only if output parameter provided, but does not handle write failures. If output path is directory without write permission or disk full, json.dumps succeeds but write_text raises PermissionError or OSError, bubbling to caller with no cleanup.
invariants.derived_from: Lines 274-276 write_text with no try/except, propagates I/O errors

invariants.statement: main (lines 284-318) calls extract_context (line 310) with no try/except. If extract_context raises (ImportError from missing llm module, RuntimeError from _parse, OSError from file I/O), exception propagates to __main__ handler (line 321-322) with no user-friendly error formatting. User sees raw Python traceback instead of actionable error message.
invariants.derived_from: Lines 310-318 extract_context call with no exception handling

understanding.purpose: Formal proof certificate generator for nine violation modes detected by pact static analysis. Each prover function (lines 81-912) encodes bug semantics as Z3 constraints, produces SAT witness demonstrating bug is real, then proves fix makes bug UNSAT (no such scenario exists). Module docstring (lines 1-12) documents three proof modes for save_without_update_fields/missing_await/optional_dereference; git history shows expansion to nine total modes (2026-05-14 feat commit). Main entry point (lines 1002-1041) accepts --mode for class proofs or --file/--line/--code/--field for instance proofs (save_without_update_fields only). Exit code 0 iff bug_sat=True ∧ fix_unsat=True (lines 1032-1037).
understanding.design_intent: Two-solver proof pattern: (1) Solver s encodes bug scenario, s.check()==sat proves bug exists, extract concrete witness values from s.model(), (2) Solver s2 encodes fix constraints, s2.check()==unsat proves fix eliminates all bug scenarios. Design decision: five provers (bare_except, mutable_default_arg, required_arg_missing, format_arg_mismatch, unvalidated_lookup_chain) guard on _HAS_Z3 (lines 454-462, 574-582, 665-673, 747-755, 832-840), return degraded certificates when Z3 absent; four provers (save_without_update_fields, missing_await, optional_dereference, llm_response_unguarded) assume Z3 imports succeed (lines 81-433) — consequence: main() hard-exits on _HAS_Z3=False (lines 1003-1008) but four provers would crash if z3 import failed. Git rationale (2026-05-21 commit): 'warn when Z3 absent rather than returning sat=True silently' — implies earlier version returned bug_sat=True when Z3 unavailable (masking inability to compute proof).
understanding.key_abstractions: visible_definitions: [ProofCertificate (lines 43-78), ProofCertificate.render (lines 52-78), prove_save_without_update_fields (lines 81-176), prove_missing_await (lines 179-268), prove_optional_dereference (lines 271-322), prove_llm_response_unguarded (lines 325-433), prove_bare_except (lines 436-555), prove_mutable_default_arg (lines 558-648), prove_required_arg_missing (lines 651-730), prove_format_arg_mismatch (lines 733-813), prove_unvalidated_lookup_chain (lines 816-912), _PROVERS (lines 915-925), InstanceCertificate (lines 928-984), InstanceCertificate.render (lines 939-984), prove_instance (lines 987-999), main (lines 1002-1041)]. ProofCertificate (dataclass, lines 43-50): mode str, bug_sat bool (True=bug real), fix_unsat bool (True=fix correct), witness dict (concrete values), axioms list[str], conclusion str. ProofCertificate.render (lines 52-78): formats certificate as text with AXIOMS/BUG SCENARIO/AFTER FIX/CONCLUSION sections, witness pretty-printed as k=v pairs (lines 67-69). _PROVERS (lines 915-925): registry mapping mode string to prover function. InstanceCertificate (lines 928-937): proof for specific source location, contains file/line/code/modified_field/class_cert. InstanceCertificate.render (lines 939-984): formats instance-specific witness (lines 963-966), suggests fix (lines 968-970), documents runtime enforcement decorator (lines 972-976), inherits verdict from class_cert (lines 979-983).
understanding.behavioral_contract: Each prover function returns ProofCertificate with (bug_sat, fix_unsat, witness, axioms, conclusion). Contract: bug_sat=True iff s.check()==sat (bug scenario satisfiable), fix_unsat=True iff s2.check()==unsat (fix eliminates all bug scenarios), witness populated only when bug_sat=True (if bug_result==sat guards at lines 122, 217, 289, 369, 502, 603, 691, 773, 871). ProofCertificate.render contract (lines 52-78): returns multiline string, never raises exception (no error handling visible). InstanceCertificate.render contract (lines 939-984): returns multiline string, verdict='PROVEN' if class_cert.bug_sat ∧ class_cert.fix_unsat else 'INCOMPLETE' (lines 979-983). main() contract (lines 1002-1041): raises SystemExit(0) if proof PROVEN, SystemExit(1) if proof INCOMPLETE or _HAS_Z3=False or no args supplied (lines 1008, 1030, 1034, 1037). prove_instance contract (lines 987-999): accepts file/line/code/modified_field, always calls prove_save_without_update_fields (line 991) — hardcoded to one mode only.
understanding.failure_modes: No try/except blocks visible except Z3 import guard (lines 21-40). Failure mode: prove_save_without_update_fields/prove_missing_await/prove_optional_dereference/prove_llm_response_unguarded (lines 81-433) assume Z3 imported successfully — if z3-solver installed but import fails (broken install, wrong Python version), _HAS_Z3=False, main() exits line 1008 before provers execute. If somehow these provers execute when z3 not imported, NameError on 'Solver', 'Int', 'Bool', etc. Failure mode: main() (lines 1003-1008) checks _HAS_Z3 but error message 'ERROR: z3-solver not installed' is misleading if z3-solver IS installed but import failed — no distinction between package absent vs import error. Failure mode: prove_instance (lines 987-999) hardcoded to mode='save_without_update_fields' (line 995) — if called with different mode in --mode arg, instance proof ignores it, always uses save_without_update_fields. Failure mode: main() (lines 1020-1023) requires ALL of file/line/code/field present for instance proof — if one arg missing, falls through to line 1029 parser.print_help(), but no validation that args.mode also absent, could print help instead of clear 'missing required arg' error.
understanding.assumptions: Assumes z3-solver package installed and importable (lines 21-40). Assumes Z3 Solver.check() returns exactly sat or unsat (lines 120, 214, 286, 366, 500, 601, 689, 771, 869 for bug_result; lines 156, 248, 304, 411, 534, 627, 711, 793, 891 for fix_result) — no timeout/unknown handling. Assumes s.model() exists when s.check()==sat (lines 123, 218, 290, 370, 503, 604, 692, 774, 872) — no guards for model extraction failure. Assumes argparse produces args with .mode/.file/.line/.code/.field attributes (lines 1020, 1024) — no hasattr guards. Assumes modified_field string in prove_instance (line 988) is valid Python identifier (used in f-string line 963, 970, 975) — no validation. Assumes sys.exit() terminates process (lines 1008, 1030, 1034, 1037) — in embedded/testing contexts where sys.exit is mocked, control may continue. Assumes ProofCertificate/InstanceCertificate dataclass fields never None (lines 44-50, 932-937) — no Optional types, but witness initialized {} (lines 121, 216, 288, 368, 501, 602, 690, 772, 870) so witness can be empty dict.
understanding.resource_obligations: No file handles, network sockets, or async visible. ProofCertificate/InstanceCertificate are dataclasses (lines 43, 928) — no __del__ or cleanup methods, no resources held. Z3 Solver objects (lines 87, 141, 186, etc.) created as local variables — Z3 C++ API manages memory via reference counting, Python GC releases on function return. No obligation to explicitly close/destroy Solvers. main() calls sys.exit() (lines 1008, 1030, 1034, 1037) — obligation: process terminates, OS reclaims all resources. Prover functions return ProofCertificate (immutable after creation) — obligation: caller may retain certificate indefinitely, no cleanup required. argparse.ArgumentParser (line 1010) holds references to args namespace — local variable, GC reclaims after main() returns/exits.

invariants.statement: Module imports z3 in try/except block (lines 21-40). When z3-solver not installed, _HAS_Z3=False (line 40). prove_bare_except (lines 454-462), prove_mutable_default_arg (lines 574-582), prove_required_arg_missing (lines 665-673), prove_format_arg_mismatch (lines 747-755), prove_unvalidated_lookup_chain (lines 832-840) all guard on _HAS_Z3 and return ProofCertificate with bug_sat=False, fix_unsat=False, empty witness, axioms=['Z3 not installed'], conclusion='Install z3-solver' when Z3 absent. Invariant: ∀ prover ∈ {bare_except, mutable_default_arg, required_arg_missing, format_arg_mismatch, unvalidated_lookup_chain}: ¬_HAS_Z3 → prover().bug_sat=False ∧ prover().fix_unsat=False.
invariants.formal: ∀ f ∈ {prove_bare_except, prove_mutable_default_arg, prove_required_arg_missing, prove_format_arg_mismatch, prove_unvalidated_lookup_chain}: ¬_HAS_Z3 → (f().bug_sat = False ∧ f().fix_unsat = False)
invariants.derived_from: Lines 454-462, 574-582, 665-673, 747-755, 832-840: if not _HAS_Z3 guards returning unverified certificates

invariants.statement: All nine prover functions (prove_save_without_update_fields lines 81-176, prove_missing_await lines 179-268, prove_optional_dereference lines 271-322, prove_llm_response_unguarded lines 325-433, prove_bare_except lines 436-555, prove_mutable_default_arg lines 558-648, prove_required_arg_missing lines 651-730, prove_format_arg_mismatch lines 733-813, prove_unvalidated_lookup_chain lines 816-912) follow identical structure: (1) create Solver s, (2) add bug scenario constraints, (3) s.check() → bug_result, (4) extract witness if SAT, (5) create Solver s2 with fix constraints, (6) s2.check() → fix_result, (7) return ProofCertificate(bug_sat=(bug_result==sat), fix_unsat=(fix_result==unsat)). Invariant: ∀ prover: prover.bug_sat=True ↔ s.check()==sat ∧ prover.fix_unsat=True ↔ s2.check()==unsat.
invariants.formal: ∀ prover ∈ PROVERS: prover() = ⟨bug_sat, fix_unsat, witness, axioms, conclusion⟩ where bug_sat ↔ (s.check() = sat) ∧ fix_unsat ↔ (s2.check() = unsat)
invariants.derived_from: All nine prover functions follow identical two-solver pattern: s for bug, s2 for fix

invariants.statement: prove_instance (lines 987-999) creates InstanceCertificate with class_cert=prove_save_without_update_fields() (line 991). InstanceCertificate.render (lines 939-984) inherits class proof verdict (lines 958-959, 980-983): verdict='PROVEN' if class_cert.bug_sat=True ∧ class_cert.fix_unsat=True, else 'INCOMPLETE'. Invariant: ∀ instance ∈ InstanceCertificate: instance.verdict = (instance.class_cert.bug_sat ∧ instance.class_cert.fix_unsat) ? 'PROVEN' : 'INCOMPLETE'.
invariants.derived_from: Lines 991, 979-983: instance proof verdict derived from class_cert.bug_sat and class_cert.fix_unsat

invariants.statement: main() (lines 1002-1041) checks args for two modes: (1) instance proof (args.file/line/code/field, lines 1020-1023) creates InstanceCertificate, ok=(inst.class_cert.bug_sat ∧ inst.class_cert.fix_unsat), (2) class proof (args.mode, lines 1024-1027) calls _PROVERS[args.mode](), ok=(cert.bug_sat ∧ cert.fix_unsat). Exit code (lines 1032-1037): ok=True → sys.exit(0), ok=False → sys.exit(1). Invariant: main() exit(0) ↔ (bug_sat=True ∧ fix_unsat=True) ∧ main() exit(1) ↔ ¬(bug_sat ∧ fix_unsat).
invariants.derived_from: Lines 1020-1037: ok computed from bug_sat ∧ fix_unsat, exit(0) if ok else exit(1)

invariants.statement: main() (lines 1002-1041) checks _HAS_Z3 at line 1003. If not _HAS_Z3, prints error to stderr (lines 1004-1006), calls sys.exit(1) (line 1008) before argument parsing. Invariant: ¬_HAS_Z3 → main() terminates with exit(1) before any prover executes. Note: prove_save_without_update_fields, prove_missing_await, prove_optional_dereference, prove_llm_response_unguarded do NOT guard on _HAS_Z3 (lines 81-433) — they assume Z3 imports succeed. Consequence: if z3-solver installed but imports fail in try block (lines 21-40), _HAS_Z3=False, main() exits, but module load succeeded.
invariants.formal: ¬_HAS_Z3 → (main() → sys.exit(1) before argparse) ∧ ∀ f ∈ {prove_save_without_update_fields, prove_missing_await, prove_optional_dereference, prove_llm_response_unguarded}: f() → z3 symbols referenced without guard
invariants.derived_from: Lines 1003-1008: main() hard-exits if _HAS_Z3=False; lines 81-433: four provers have no _HAS_Z3 guard

invariants.statement: All prover functions that reach bug_result==sat (lines 122-132, 217-225, 289-294, 369-383, 502-510, 603-610, 691-697, 773-779, 871-878) extract witness from s.model(). Witness extraction pattern: if bug_result == sat: m = s.model(); witness = {k: m[var] for k, var in mapping}. Only prove_llm_response_unguarded (lines 369-383) adds computed fields to witness beyond m[var] queries (line 371: 'trigger' computed via if/else chain from m[content_filtered]/m[api_error]/m[stream_truncated], lines 382-383: 'seen in prod?' static string). Invariant: ∀ prover: bug_result=sat → witness populated from s.model(); witness fields are Z3 var evaluations except prove_llm_response_unguarded adds 2 derived fields.
invariants.formal: ∀ prover: (prover.bug_result = sat) → (prover.witness = {k: prover.s.model()[v] | (k,v) ∈ var_mapping}) ∪ derived_fields where |derived_fields| ≤ 2
